emergencyProcedures.title: Emergency Engine Shutdown on the Ground
Emergency Ground Egress
steps: CFS handle - Rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and pull (BOTH)
Upper fittings, lower fittings, and leg restraint garters - Release (BOTH)
BAT, GEN, and AUX BAT switches - OFF

emergencyProcedures.title: Immediate Airstart (PMU NORM)
steps: Engine instruments - Monitor ITT, N1, and oil pressure
PCL - As required after N1 reaches IDLE RPM (approximately 67% N1)

emergencyProcedures.title: Uncommanded Power Changes / Loss of Power / Uncommanded Propeller Feather
steps: PROP SYS CIRCUIT BREAKER - PULL, IF NP STABLE BELOW 40%
PROP SYS circuit breaker - Reset, as required

source.label: NATOPS
source.location: Uncommanded Power Changes / Loss of Power / Uncommanded Propeller Feather

emergencyProcedures.title: Fire In Flight
emergencyProcedures.note: If fire is not confirmed, execute PEL. If fire is extinguished, execute a forced landing.

emergencyProcedures.title: Smoke and Fume Elimination / Electrical Fire
steps: OBOGS - CHECK (BOTH): supply lever ON, concentration lever MAX, pressure lever EMERGENCY
Descend below 10,000 feet MSL - Initiate (as required)
CFS - Rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise and pull (if necessary)

source.label: NATOPS
source.location: Smoke and Fume Elimination / Electrical Fire

emergencyProcedures.title: Chip Detector Warning
steps: PCL - Minimum necessary to intercept ELP; avoid unnecessary PCL movements

emergencyProcedures.title: Oil System Malfunction or Low Oil Pressure

source.label: NATOPS
source.location: Oil System Malfunction or Low Oil Pressure

emergencyProcedures.title: Forced Landing
steps: Airspeed - 125 KIAS prior to extending landing gear
EMER LDG GR handle - Pull (as required)
Airspeed - 120 KIAS minimum until intercepting final; 110 KIAS minimum on final

emergencyProcedures.title: Precautionary Emergency Landing (PEL)
steps: Climb or accelerate to intercept ELP

limits.label: VMO / MMO
limits.value: 316 KIAS up to and including 19,020 feet MSL; 0.67 IMN above 19,020 feet MSL.

source.label: NATOPS
source.location: Maximum Operating Airspeed / Maximum Operating Mach Number

limits.label: Flaps Limit
limits.value: VFE is 150 KIAS.

limits.label: Landing Gear Limit
limits.value: VLE is 150 KIAS.

limits.label: Turbulent Air
limits.value: VG is 207 KIAS; recommended turbulent airspeed is 180 KIAS.

limits.label: Operating Maneuvering Speed
limits.value: VO is 227 KIAS; full rudder deflection above 150 KIAS exceeds rudder control system limits.

limits.label: Zero G Limit
limits.value: Zero G is limited to 5 seconds maximum.

limits.label: Negative G Limits
limits.value: Negative G operation, including inverted, is limited to 60 seconds. Additional negative G maneuvers require 60 seconds upright flight between maneuvers.

limits.label: Prohibited Maneuvers
limits.value: Inverted stalls, inverted spins, aggravated spins past two turns, spins with PCL above idle, spins with landing gear, flaps, or speed brake extended, spins with PMU off, and spins below 10,000 feet pressure altitude are prohibited.

limits.label: Weight Limits
limits.value: Maximum ramp 6,950 pounds; takeoff 6,900 pounds; landing 6,900 pounds; zero fuel 5,850 pounds; baggage compartment 80 pounds.

limits.label: Touchdown Sink Rate
limits.value: 600 feet per minute at normal landing tire pressure; 780 feet per minute at maximum landing tire pressure.

limits.label: Wind Limits
limits.value: Maximum crosswind component: dry runway 25 knots, wet runway 10 knots, icy runway 5 knots. Maximum tailwind component for takeoff: 10 knots.

limits.label: Fuel Imbalance
limits.value: Maximum lateral fuel imbalance is 50 pounds.

limits.label: Ground Temperature Limits
limits.value: Ground operation is limited to ambient temperatures from -23 C to +43 C. Ground operation above +43 C is permitted for up to 15 minutes only to taxi the aircraft to park.

limits.label: Generator Limits
limits.value: Inflight generator limits: +50 to -2 AMPS. Ground / inflight voltage: 28.0 to 28.5 volts.

emergencyProcedures.title: Abnormal Start
steps: CONDITION LEVER - FUEL CUTOFF (DEC BELOW 790 C)
STARTER - STARTER ONLY (FOR THE REMAINDER OF THE 40 SECONDS)

emergencyProcedures.title: Smoke and Fume Elimination
steps: OXYGEN MASK / MIC SWITCHES (100 PERCENT) - AS REQUIRED

emergencyProcedures.title: Emergency Shutdown on Deck
steps: STOP THE AIRCRAFT AND SET THE PARKING BRAKE

emergencyProcedures.title: Primary Governor Failure / Malfunction
steps: ATTEMPT TO ADJUST PROP RPM TO NORMAL OPERATING RANGE
ALTERNATE PROP FEATHER CHECKLIST - AS REQUIRED

emergencyProcedures.title: Explosive Decompression
steps: OXYGEN MASK / MIC SWITCHES (100 PERCENT) - AS REQUIRED

emergencyProcedures.title: Unscheduled Electric Trim Activation
steps: A/P / TRIM DISCONNECT (control wheel) - DEPRESS FULLY AND HOLD

emergencyProcedures.title: Spin / Out of Control Flight Recovery
steps: RUDDER - FULL DEFLECTION OPPOSITE DIRECTION OF TURN NEEDLE
CONTROL WHEEL - PULL OUT OF DIVE BY EXERTING SMOOTH, STEADY BACK PRESSURE

source.label: Ops Limits Study Sheet
source.location: Spin / Out of Control Flight Recovery

emergencyProcedures.title: Terrain Warning (IMC or at Night)
steps: PITCH - AS REQUIRED TO SET AND MAINTAIN VX
CONTINUE CLIMB AT VX UNTIL ALL VISUAL AND VOICE WARNINGS CEASE

emergencyProcedures.title: Single-Engine Waveoff / Missed Approach
steps: POWER - MAX CONTINUOUS, ESTABLISH POSITIVE RATE OF CLIMB (VXSE MINIMUM)

emergencyProcedures.title: Low Altitude Windshear
steps: PITCH - SET AND HOLD APPROXIMATELY 15 DEGREES NOSEUP

emergencyProcedures.title: Waveoff / Missed Approach
steps: POWER - AS REQUIRED, ESTABLISH POSITIVE RATE OF CLIMB (VX MINIMUM)

emergencyProcedures.title: Smoke / Fire of Unknown Origin
steps: OXYGEN MASKS / MIC SWITCHES (100%) - AS REQUIRED
ALL ELECTRICAL SWITCHES (EXCEPT AUX BATT) - OFF

limits.label: Engine Operating Limits
limits.value: Maximum allowable 5 minutes: 1,315 ft-lb torque at 2,200 RPM or 1,520 ft-lb at 1,900 RPM, ITT 790, N1 101.5%, prop 2,200 RPM, oil pressure 85-100 PSI, oil temp 10-99 C.

limits.label: Fuel Capacity
limits.value: Total fuel capacity is 387.6 U.S. gallons, of which 384 U.S. gallons are usable.

limits.label: Normal TAS Altitude Range
limits.value: Plus or minus 2,700 feet, flared landings only.

limits.label: Touchdown Sink Rate
limits.value: Maximum sink rate at ground contact is 600 feet per minute.

limits.label: Crosswind Limit
limits.value: Maximum crosswind component is 20 knots.

limits.label: Gyro Suction
limits.value: Normal operating range is 4.3 to 5.9 inHg.

limits.label: Minimum Oxygen Required
limits.value: Local / cross-country flight minimum oxygen is 1,000 / 1,500 PSI.

limits.label: Cabin Pressure Differential
limits.value: Maximum operating cabin pressure differential is 4.7 PSI.

limits.label: VMCG
limits.value: Minimum controllable speed on ground is 63 KIAS.

limits.label: Flap G Limits
limits.value: Full flaps: +2.0 to 0.0 G.

limits.label: Pneumatic Pressure
limits.value: Normal operating range is 12 to 20 PSI.

limits.label: Weight Limits
limits.value: Maximum ramp 9,710 pounds; maximum takeoff 9,650 pounds; maximum landing 9,168 pounds.

limits.label: Climb and Glide Speeds
limits.value: VYSE 110 KIAS, max range glide 130 KIAS, max endurance glide 102 KIAS, VX 102 KIAS, VY 108 KIAS, VXSE 102 KIAS.

limits.label: Gear and Flap Limits
limits.value: VLE 155 KIAS. Landing gear retraction 145 KIAS. VFE approach 174 KIAS. VFE full 140 KIAS.

limits.label: Single-Engine and Maneuvering Speeds
limits.value: VSSE 91 KIAS, VMCA 86 KIAS, VA 153 KIAS.

limits.label: VMO / MMO
limits.value: VMO is 227 KIAS. Decrease VMO 4 KIAS for every 1,000 feet above 15,500 feet. MMO is 0.48 Mach.

limits.label: Battery Voltage Minimums
limits.value: Minimum battery voltage for APU charge is 18 VDC, APU start is 20 VDC, and battery start is 22 VDC.

limits.label: Starter Cycle Limits
limits.value: Starter duty cycle is limited to three 40-second cycles. Cooling periods: first cycle 60 seconds, second cycle 60 seconds, third cycle 30 minutes.

limits.label: Prop Deicer Ammeter
limits.value: Normal operation is 14 to 18 amps.

limits.label: Acceleration Limits
limits.value: Clean: +3.0 to -1.0 G.

limits.label: Prohibited Maneuvers
limits.value: Intentional spins are prohibited.

emergencyProcedures.title: Loss of Directional Control
steps: Gear and flaps - DO NOT REPOSITION
Refer to procedure for blown tire(s) or NWS / NWS AUG caution light

emergencyProcedures.title: Fuel Leak / Excessive Fuel Flow
steps: Throttle - AS REQUIRED (maintain a minimum of 70 percent N2)

emergencyProcedures.title: Vibrations
steps: Altitude - DESCEND BELOW 10,000 FEET IF POSSIBLE
AIR FLOW knob - NORMAL / AS DESIRED
Request inspection by other aircraft, if practical

emergencyProcedures.title: Electrical Fire
steps: Proceed to VMC as soon as possible
Plan a no-flap approach and arrested landing, if available

emergencyProcedures.title: Smoke or Fumes in Cockpit
steps: Initiate controlled descent to below 18,000 feet MSL
AIR FLOW knob - OFF (below 18,000 feet MSL if possible)
Warn other cockpit occupant / secure loose items

emergencyProcedures.title: Rapid Decompression
steps: Remain on emergency oxygen until depleted and then resume OBOGS operation until engine shutdown

emergencyProcedures.title: Total Electrical Failure
steps: Proceed to VMC as soon as possible
Attempt to reset generator / check BATT switches - ON
Make a short field arrestment if available

limits.label: Solo Flying
limits.value: Solo flying shall be conducted only from the forward cockpit.

limits.label: Altitude Limit
limits.value: Maximum altitude is 41,000 feet MSL.

limits.label: Icing
limits.value: Flight in known icing conditions shall be avoided. When airframe icing is visible, intentional stalls or use of full flaps is not authorized.

limits.label: Engine Handling Limits
limits.value: Throttle shall be at idle for abrupt pulls to full back stick, abrupt full lateral stick inputs at full back stick, and airspeeds less than 85 KIAS above 15,000 feet MSL.

limits.label: Engine Operating Limits
limits.value: MRT: 104 percent N2 / 610 C for 30 minutes per flight hour. Transient: 106 percent N2 / 645 C for less than 20 seconds. Maximum continuous: 100 percent N2 / 550 C. Idle: 56.5 +/- 2 percent N2 / 450 C.

limits.label: GTS Start Limits
limits.value: GTS start with external power is prohibited. Minimum interval between each GTS start attempt is 3 minutes. After three consecutive starts, wait 30 minutes before any further start attempts.

limits.label: Fuel Temperature Limits
limits.value: For A++(F-24), Jet A, and A++100 (F-27), do not allow indicated TAT to dwell at or below the fuel freeze point plus 3 C. Operation is restricted to ambient temperatures above 0 C when using fuel without FSII.

limits.label: System Airspeed Limits
limits.value: Landing gear transition / extended: 200 KIAS or less. Flaps / slats transition / extended: 200 KIAS or less. Arresting hook transition / extended: 450 KIAS or less.

limits.label: Canopy and Deck Limits
limits.value: Canopy full open for taxi: prevailing wind 20 knots or less. Stationary or towing with canopy full open: prevailing wind 32 knots or less. Tires and nosewheel steering on deck: 176 knots groundspeed or less.

limits.label: Hot Refueling
limits.value: Hot refueling fuel level must be 2,800 pounds or less if PRECHECK is unsuccessful.

limits.label: Crosswind Limits
limits.value: Single aircraft dry runway 20 knots, wet runway 15 knots, section takeoff 10 knots, banner tow 10 knots, NWS off / failed 15 knots.

limits.label: High Gain Nosewheel Steering
limits.value: High gain nosewheel steering shall be used for low speed taxi operations only, less than 10 knots groundspeed.

limits.label: FCLP Landing Configurations
limits.value: FCLP landings are authorized only with clean loading and full flaps, or pylons alone / pylons with empty PMBRs or BRU-38A and full flaps.

limits.label: Landing Sink Rate
limits.value: For landings in a configuration other than authorized FCLP configurations, sink rate shall not exceed 600 feet per minute.

limits.label: Banner Tow Limits
limits.value: Maximum altitude 20,000 feet MSL. Maximum airspeed 220 KIAS. Maximum bank angle 45 degrees. Maximum AOA 18 units.

limits.label: Banner Release Limits
limits.value: Minimum banner release altitude 500 feet AGL. Banner release airspeed 140 to 200 KIAS. For release between 140 and 150 KIAS, use one-half flaps.

limits.label: Prohibited Maneuvers
limits.value: Intentional spins or tailslides are prohibited. Sustained zero or negative G flight for more than 30 seconds is prohibited.
